name: Decision Authority Compatibility
allowed_use_boundary: Use as compatibility evidence for mapping public wiki decision vocabulary to ST-004 authority values.
Do not treat as certification, endorsement, execution authority, or commit-time authority.
claims: The repo-standards decision-authority schema defines canonical ST-004 authority values.
Wiki public vocabulary can be mapped into those values for explanatory consistency.
non_claims: This mapping does not grant execution authority.
This mapping does not prove admissibility.
This mapping does not replace policy, delegation, evidence, or validation.

transition_table_mapping.claims: public wiki values map to canonical ST-004 authority values
transition_table_mapping.non_claims: no execution authority, admissibility proof, or commit-time standing
transition_table_mapping.actor_or_authority_model: repo-standards defines canonical vocabulary; wiki records public compatibility only
transition_table_mapping.evidence_model: source schema reference plus local compatibility record
transition_table_mapping.decision_or_result_model: ALLOW to allowed; DENY to denied; FAIL-CLOSED to fail-closed
transition_table_mapping.reconstruction_model: source schema plus wiki mapping reconstruct the vocabulary relationship
transition_table_mapping.SPE_overlap: authority vocabulary can support standing review but does not create standing
transition_table_mapping.StegVerse_ecosystem_overlap: decision vocabulary reconciliation across repo-standards and wiki public doctrine
transition_table_mapping.fail_closed_conditions: missing canonical schema, unmapped value, or authority overclaim

fail_closed_conditions: any certification, endorsement, execution authority, or commit-time authority claim appears
